actions.editDescription: The name is visible everywhere (sidebar, project list, etc.).
actions.descriptionPlaceholder: Internal note, matter no., opposing party…
actions.deleteDescription: “{name}” will be deleted. Conversations and documents will be detached but kept — you will find them back in their original location.

documentsPanel.heading: Documents
documentsPanel.overlayLabel: Drop to add to this project
documentsPanel.overlayHint: PDF, DOCX or text — 25 MB max per file
documentsPanel.empty: Drag your files here, or import them — they will be attached to this folder.

addDialog.chooseFolderError: Choose an existing folder or create a new one.
addDialog.title: New project
addDialog.description: A project groups the conversations and documents of a matter. Choose where its files will be stored.
addDialog.namePlaceholder: e.g. Dupont matter · Commercial lease dispute
addDialog.descriptionPlaceholder: Internal note, matter no., opposing party…
addDialog.storageHint: The project's documents — and what Louis will consider in RAG — are those filed in this folder and its subfolders.

detail.emptyConversations: Start a conversation attached to this folder to keep its history in one place.

collaborators.heading: Collaborators
collaborators.emptyManage: Add colleagues from your firm to work together on this matter.

list.title: Projects.
list.helpBody: A project groups the conversations and documents of a client matter, and restricts the AI's reasoning to that scope only.
list.intro: Group conversations and documents around a client matter, a case, a theme.
list.aside: A project is a shared container: conversations, documents and, soon, enabled connectors are gathered there. Especially suited to following a client matter from end to end.
list.emptyBody: A project groups the conversations and documents tied to the same client matter. You keep track of the whole case rather than a series of isolated conversations.
